Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria), hypoglycemia with low HbA1c, and abnormal urinalysis (cloudy, brown urine, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity). No evidence of urinary infection. Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney disease with glycosuria unrelated to hyperglycemia (possibly renal glycosuria or tubular dysfunction). Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.
